entries.title: Recursive and deeply nested embeds stop with a notice instead of freezing or expanding forever
Named workspaces persist and switch the complete window context without writing graph files
entries.notes: Model A keeps one window per graph and swaps the complete existing PersistedSession in place. The Rust registry test proves crash-safe idempotent migration and byte/mtime identity for graph pages across the registry CRUD cycle; frontend tests prove routed split-layout restoration, one window-level mount, and no graph-writer calls. WebKitGTK placement and hover feel still require real-app confirmation.

entries.title: On a narrow or mobile viewport, top-bar actions stay on-screen while workspaces live in the sidebar with a compact fallback when it is closed
entries.notes: GH #205. The full workspace label moved from the no-wrap toolbar to a sidebar header; the toolbar retains only a compact W fallback when that sidebar is not mounted. A topbar inline-size container query keeps sidebar, search, Settings, and native window controls visible while calendar, journals, theme, right sidebar, and history actions use one transient-layer overflow menu below the available-width threshold. The threshold is 460px inline-size, chosen so the fixed clusters (which need ~364px) stay visible whenever they fit — the earlier 760px value over-collapsed and hid actions with the toolbar half empty.

entries.title: The top-bar overflow ("…") menu closes when the user clicks anywhere outside it
entries.notes: GH #205 follow-up (Martin report). The overflow popover registered a transient layer (Escape/Back) but had no outside-click handler, so a click elsewhere left it open. A capture-phase mousedown outside the menu root now dismisses it without restoring trigger focus.

entries.title: Reference-group collapse state survives a result refresh while its cleanup no longer re-walks the whole subtree on unrelated descendant edits
Only the page-actions trigger that owns the open menu reports aria-expanded
Every ordinary page exposes one accessible keyboard-operable canonical actions menu
Sub-640 px sidebars are modal overlay drawers with safe dismissal
Transient semantic parent branches dismiss their newest live child before their parent
Bare slash defaults to Page reference and continues into an empty page picker
Page and tag completion use the OG adaptive default while preserving explicit existing and typed policies
Mod-L inserts parser-aware Markdown and Org external links through every editor command surface
Linked and unlinked references render in side panels
Page-property backlinks render their linkified value in References, not raw key:: value
Native form controls retain Tab traversal while outline editors keep indentation ownership
Reference results include alias mentions and explicit links in page properties
Bare tags and alias property values contribute canonical Linked References
Mixed-case page links route to the existing canonical page across navigation surfaces
macOS window decorations do not duplicate native-looking corners

coverage.exemption: macOS-only appearance remains a recorded native-platform gap; shared chrome structure is render-tested.

entries.title: macOS does not repeatedly request graph-folder permission

coverage.exemption: The sandbox permission prompt requires a signed macOS application and cannot be reproduced on Linux CI.

entries.title: Multiline nodes do not inherit first-line formatting
Underscores render without unintended emphasis
Journal references use chronological rather than modified order
Custom page properties render wiki-link values
Calendar Today resolves the current day at click time
Entering a sheet cell does not shift its layout
Typed Table cell values commit before Tab navigation and remain visible during the next edit
Multiline paste inside a code fence stays in the block
A top-level block can be deleted without corrupting siblings
Templates can be found and inserted
Deleting a page-link opener does not leave unmatched brackets
Windows New page button creates and opens a page
Org block IDs remain hidden property drawers
macOS Command-key wheel tails do not change text size
Closing the Linux app does not crash WebKit subprocesses
Opening All pages avoids unsafe emoji renderer crashes
Clicking right of a trailing page link places the caret after it
The Linux welcome window exposes a working close control
Org hidden properties stay hidden while editing
Journal feed scrolls without requiring a window resize
Shift-clicking a linked page opens the sidebar without selecting text
Windows clipboard screenshots paste as image bytes
Enter on a zoomed leaf creates a child
Android does not offer the unsupported desktop updater
About-page links open through the platform link handler

Deleting a journal day leaves one editable block
Inline iframe dimensions honor sanitized style attributes

The Calculator slash command activates the live calculator immediately
Linked references preserve task markers

PDF area highlights use the OG platform modifier and post-drag color choice
entries.notes: All-platform component coverage plus direct Linux WebKit native proof of Shift and toolbar entry, chooser cancellation/no-write, mode reset, compatible persistence, and reopen. Direct Windows/macOS native gestures remain unverified in this packet.

entries.title: PDF readers expose persistent light warm and dark themes plus nested outline destination navigation
entries.notes: All-platform component coverage plus direct Linux WebKit native proof of accessible light, warm, and dark selection, explicit and named destinations, and a same-XDG process relaunch proving theme persistence. Direct Windows/macOS native presentation remains unverified in this packet.

entries.title: PDF uploads highlights annotation pages and restored view state match Logseq OG while retaining Tine filename templates
entries.notes: Direct literal upload, selection, clipboard, sidecar/HLS and reopen proof is Linux WebKit/GTK; component and Rust contracts cover shared cross-platform behavior. Windows/macOS native upload and chooser surfaces remain tentative.

Opening an existing graph routes quick capture without relying on a later OS focus event

Published outline guides align with bullets and block embeds avoid duplicated connectors
Mobile pages reserve most of the narrow viewport for outline text

Search results separate context from bounded excerpts and highlight all actual positive matches
Graph search can open in a session-backed virtual query tab and materialize safely when named

Friendly and advanced query frontends preserve predicates and explain diagnostics and match evidence
Scrollable panes share quiet theme-aware scrollbar styling with accessible platform fallbacks

Help improve Tine rejects oracle-artifact reports after canonical JSON normalization
Android status and navigation bar icon appearance follows the active Tine theme
Deep mobile outlines preserve usable text width, aligned guides, and separate trailing fold targets without stealing leaf text taps
The tab-overflow menu reorders the pane canonical tab order by pointer and keyboard
Selection toolbar wraps and unwraps page links and inline code without losing selection
Linked and unlinked references expose one bounded canonical occurrence-evidence contract with Logseq-compatible edge rules
Large reference results use bounded exact-hit excerpts and accessible page-group disclosure
Ctrl K uses favorites and device-local frecency only to break ties within objective relevance classes
Tine exposes one consistent visible search trigger plus Ctrl K across layouts
Ctrl Enter cycles every selected block independently in one atomic undoable update
Opening fenced code blocks offer bounded language autocomplete without changing bare-fence Enter
Linux Developer Tools detach after attachment on X11/XWayland while native Wayland stays usable docked
Page property settings preserve literal header structure from UI action through guarded save and reload
Canonical Markdown page-header properties use the ordinary editor and persist without an outline bullet
Re-saving a page whose header is the flagless properties-only first bullet never jams the save queue with a page-preamble refusal

The frameless Quick Capture surface has a visible theme-aware border

Plasma Wayland task switchers resolve Tine's application icon instead of a generic placeholder
Reference-count badges refresh after a copied block reference is saved
Every visible UUID reference refreshes after loaded or unloaded source edits and deletion

A Linux secondary launch can hand off Quick Capture without an Xlib/XCB thread abort

entries.notes: Blocking graph-ownership repair. Proof is semantic filesystem state, not debounce timing or viewer pixels.

entries.title: QueryBuilder popovers dismiss before their Advanced modal parent without changing query drafts
Formula value pickers dismiss one active child before their editor without changing the formula draft
Selection formatting keeps browser-selected outer whitespace outside Markdown and Org delimiters
Inner pane overscroll never rubber-bands fixed application chrome
A line-leading inline-code property lookalike remains visible code rather than hidden metadata
Graph-global Recent follows foreground page activation without promoting background or restored tabs
Children-backed Sheet field rename migrates schema rows and dependent expressions atomically
Linked References combine bounded content search with canonical include and exclude facets
Linked-reference and query hits show one bounded ordered ancestor breadcrumb
Reference and query trees initialize bounded view-local disclosure without mutating source collapse
Search treats canonically equivalent Unicode page names aliases and block text as identical without accent folding
entries.notes: Catalog-derived OG parity finding. Scope is lowercase plus NFC only: regex source bytes remain unchanged and ASCII cafe must not match cafe with an acute accent. Linux native WebDriver proves page/alias/block equivalence, full-grapheme evidence, exact Create suppression, and the accent-folding negative.

entries.title: Shift-only Enter opens selected Ctrl K page and block results in the right sidebar
entries.notes: Catalog-derived OG parity finding; Linux native WebDriver proves literal Shift-only Enter for page and block results while the main route remains unchanged. Mod-Enter transfer and path-addressed duplicate-page sidebar identity are explicitly excluded.

entries.title: An explicitly selected physical page remains the edit owner through follow-on navigation, parked surfaces, file actions, and persisted reopen
entries.notes: BC-H1 high repair on pinned base d4b9a282d6ec95310d51bd061412ba805ef50009. Fail-before command: ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/router.test.ts src/router.recent.test.ts src/session.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts --reporter=verbose (3 causal failures, 37 passing). Known-owner transitions retain one graph-relative PageTarget/BlockTarget; stale or ambiguous mutation targets fail closed. The name-keyed working set may expose one same-name physical owner. Legacy DSL RefGroups, typed links, Favorites, calendar, Guide, and other intentionally logical routes remain pathless.

entries.title: Mod Shift K searches only blocks owned by the focused routed page
entries.notes: Linux native WebDriver proves a collapsed current-page descendant, exclusion of the same query on another page and all global providers, plus no-single-page fallback to global search. Path-aware page identity remains covered below the native layer.

entries.title: An installed plugin with an incompatible manifest remains removable by its real package identity
A cached signed revocation prevents persisted plugin and theme activation before a bounded live refresh
entries.notes: Causal JS/native tests cover one signed envelope, guarded split-key migration, absent versus unsafe startup, activation hold/release, durable cached/live disable with retry, failed-publication last-good preservation, and generation/settings races. The production-key-signed native journey proves atomic migration and restart non-execution and passed three consecutive unchanged-binary release-mode runs on f688b746 (13.1s, 13.2s, 13.1s); it is stable and included in linux-release.

entries.title: Asynchronous plugin results stay owned by the graph and editor generation that invoked them
entries.notes: Exact graph/effect ownership prevents a delayed graph-A command, slash completion, or decoration response from mutating or focusing an equal UUID/raw entity in graph B. On frozen d4b9a282, the causal deferred manager test failed with graph B raw 'A result' instead of 'same raw'. Deterministic unit/render proof is green; the native journey passed three consecutive unchanged-binary release-mode runs on d75f747e (4.9s, 4.8s, 4.8s) and is stable in linux-release.

entries.title: A signed theme installs when its validated mode set matches regardless of canonical key order
Mouse side buttons X1 and X2 navigate page history back and forward
entries.notes: Native mouse side-button navigation (GH #156). WebKitGTK/WebView2 do not deliver X1/X2 to the web layer as auxclick (physical test on fcc8079 confirmed the DOM-only listener was dead in Tine though fine in Firefox), so native_mouse_history.rs intercepts them: Linux GDK button-press 8/9 via with_webview connect_event (stop propagation), Windows WM_APPCOMMAND via window subclassing; both emit a history-navigate Tauri event driving the existing goBack/goForward. The DOM auxclick listener remains a fallback with a 100ms native+DOM de-dup, filtered by window label. Linux PHYSICALLY CONFIRMED by the maintainer on deployed 688a654 (2026-07-18); the Windows path is implemented but unverified on Windows hardware. Unit test covers the DOM/bridge dispatch; the native handler's real proof is the physical test.

entries.title: Pasted block text keeps literal square brackets instead of backslash-escaping them
entries.notes: Martin internal report. Turndown (HTML->Markdown structured paste) escaped Markdown punctuation in text nodes, so copying '[ref]' pasted as backslash-bracket. Fixed by overriding the Turndown instance text escaper to identity in src/editor/htmlPaste.ts; structure (headings/lists/links/code) still emitted by dedicated rules. Fail-before proven in htmlPaste.test.tsx (multi-block outline raw was backslash-escaped before). Accepted side effect: external HTML text literally containing Markdown like double-asterisk bold now round-trips as Markdown rather than escaped literal, which is correct for Tine whose block raw IS Markdown.

entries.title: Deleting a block selection or returning to a pane keeps a block selected and keyboard-navigable
entries.notes: Martin internal report. deleteSelection() and the pane-select return paths (activate/dismiss) unconditionally cleared the block selection, and Arrow block-nav is gated on hasSelection(), so from empty there was no keyboard way back in. Now deleteSelection re-selects a survivor (next visible, else previous, else parent, else clear) and pane-return restores the remembered block (else the focused pane's first visible block). Fail-before proven by manager: 2 store + 2 keybindings tests fail on pre-fix code, pass after; sibling pane-select suite unaffected. Real-app keyboard feel confirmed separately on the deployed binary.

entries.title: Linked/Unlinked References match Logseq: NFC page/alias identity, plain property text as unlinked, same-name merge, real title beats alias, and visible truncation/error
entries.notes: GH #137 matched to LEGACY OG (Logseq) source behavior (not the DB-rewrite master), per Martin's directive to match OG not preference. Page/alias identity = lowercase+NFC+boundary-slash (refs::page_key), applied to aliases and to scoped cache invalidation via a shared real-page-first resolver; plain unbracketed property text (including page-level front matter) counts as Unlinked; same-basename/different-path pages merge into one group keyed by normalized name; a real title is not shadowed by another page's alias and duplicate alias owners resolve by deterministic path sort; panels surface truncation (showing N of M) and a bounded error instead of an empty panel. Adversarially verified (Sol/xhigh, 2 rounds: 3 blockers found+fixed, re-verified CLEAR). Follow-up (non-blocking): an older rejected reference request can set a stale loadError label after a newer success (request-sequencing race).

entries.title: Autocomplete and Ctrl+K rank all matching blocks globally before capping, so a strong match is never omitted by graph traversal order
entries.notes: GH #186. Page/tag candidates were already a global BinaryHeap top-k, but block-ref production walked pages in traversal order and broke at the cap with a flat score, so a strong block match on a late page was silently omitted. Fixed: query_plan::execute_blocks builds a global bounded-heap top-k with real per-block relevance (match-class > word-boundary > earlier-offset > shorter-text > fewer-occurrences, deterministic tie-break by (page rel_path, in-page index) — bare traversal index was nondeterministic because walk_page_files reads fs::read_dir unsorted; regression block_search_topk_ties_sort_rel_path_with_reversed_page_snapshot forces a reversed page snapshot); OG caps adopted (inline pages 100 / block-refs 20; Ctrl+K 100/100 ranked pool, existing chunked presentation); autocomplete fold NFKC->NFC to match #137/backend. Fail-before proven (best block on last page survives + ranks ahead of weaker earlier matches; NFC-vs-NFKC ordering). Manager-reviewed block top-k + green (tine-core 455, autocomplete 50, component 20); not separately adversarially verified (search-ordering, lower risk than #137).

entries.title: Settings panel scrolls on narrow/mobile viewports so every setting is reachable

coverage.exemption: Martin internal report (Android Settings not scrollable). CSS-layout fix: in the mobile column layout (.settings-modal flex-direction:column at max-width:480px) .settings-pane lacked min-height:0, so it grew to its content height and .settings-pane-body's overflow-y:auto scroll never engaged, leaving lower settings clipped by the modal's overflow:hidden — the textbook flexbox min-height:auto scroll bug. Fixed by adding min-height:0 to .settings-pane and .settings-pane-body. jsdom cannot exercise CSS layout, and headless-Chromium navigation to Settings in the mobile drawer layout could not be automated here (confirmed the app DOES enter the mobile column layout at narrow viewports). Needs mobile-viewport/device confirmation, pending with the other Android items (#192, M-B).

entries.title: The mobile sidebar drawer closes on a horizontal swipe toward its edge, distinct from a vertical scroll
entries.notes: Martin internal report (M-B). MobileDrawerPanel now has touchstart/move/end handlers that, on the first ~10px of movement, distinguish a horizontal drag toward the drawer's closed edge (left drawer -> drag left; right -> drag right) from a vertical scroll or an opening-ward drag; a close-ward drag past max(56px, 30% of panel width) calls dismiss('back'). Synthetic-touch test proves a close-ward swipe dismisses while vertical, short, and opening-ward drags do not (red pre-fix: no touch handlers existed). Live finger-following animation deferred as polish. The touch FEEL still needs mobile-device confirmation.

entries.title: Accepting a page/block-ref completion while editing inside an existing ref rewrites the whole ref, leaving no stray closing bracket (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: GH #199. Editing inside an already-closed [[word]] (e.g. autopaired [[second]], arrow-left, type 'first ' -> [[first |second]]) and accepting a completion left a stray ]] ([[first]]second]]) because Block.tsx replaceTrigger only swallowed the closing pair when it sat IMMEDIATELY at the caret (ref.value.slice(t.end,t.end+2)===pair). OG-verified (og@6e7afa8, findings in tine-agents specs notes): OG's query range is [[->caret (matches Tine's detectTrigger, unchanged), but on accept OG scans forward from the caret to the ref's closer (:end-pattern ]]), strips it (postfix-fn) and rewrites the whole region. Fix: new pure refCompletionEnd(value,end,insertedText) in autocomplete.ts scans forward on the CURRENT LINE for the closer matching the completion's suffix (]]/))/}}), returning just past it or the caret when none ahead; replaceTrigger now uses it. Covers page AND block refs (same latent defect) and the latent end-caret ]]]] doubling. Not a regression: latent since the autocomplete feature shipped (e54ad36e, 2026-06-14). Fail-before proven (pre-fix immediate-only range yields [[first]]second]]); 7 unit cases (mid/end/unclosed/block/line-bounded/non-ref/reporter-integration); Block.referenceAuthoring + tagAutocomplete + selectionWrap render tests still green (wiring regression guard). Delegated to gpt-5.6-terra (xhigh), manager-reviewed + gates run. Real-app editor confirmation pending on next deploy (pure-logic fix, proportionate).

entries.title: Linked References hide the per-block mention count and occurrence-jump buttons unless a block mentions the page more than once (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: GH #200. Each backlink block rendered Tine's OccurrenceControls (ReferenceEvidence.tsx) always-on (LiveRefGroup.tsx + reference excerpt rows): a '{n} mention(s)' label plus one numbered occurrence-jump button per mention. For a single-mention block (the common case) this is a redundant '1 mention' + lone '1' button, which the reporter found confusing. OG (logseq@6e7afa8) shows NEITHER a per-block mention count nor occurrence-jump buttons in Linked References (only the separate ((block))-ref-count badge, which Tine also has and which correctly shows nothing for a non-block-referenced block). Per Martin's decision (option B), OccurrenceControls now renders only when occurrences.length>1 — removing the redundancy for single mentions while keeping jump-to-occurrence where multiple mentions make it useful. Render test: 1 mention -> no .reference-occurrence-controls / no 'mention' text; 3 mentions -> '3 mentions' + 3 jump buttons. Fail-before proven (pre-fix rendered controls unconditionally). LinkedReferences/UnlinkedReferences/LiveRefGroup render tests green.

entries.title: A Show brackets setting and mod-c mod-b shortcut toggle whether page-reference [[ ]] brackets render, persisted in config.edn (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (settings.show-brackets, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (logseq@6e7afa8) has :ui/show-brackets? in logseq/config.edn defaulting true (state.cljs:652); shortcut :ui/toggle-brackets = mod+c mod+b (shortcut/config.cljs:362) flips it; a Show brackets appearance toggle (settings.cljs:209); page-reference render gates the .bracket spans on state/show-brackets? (block.cljs:759/973/1193), with nested-link outer and the contents page and html-export as exceptions. Tine had no such setting/shortcut and always rendered brackets (inline.tsx:310). Implemented full parity mirroring :feature/enable-timetracking? end-to-end (config.rs field+writer, model.rs GraphMeta, commands.rs/lib.rs command, backend.ts/mock.ts, ui.ts showBrackets/changeShowBrackets, inline.tsx render gate, keybindings.ts, Settings.tsx toggle). Fail-before: showBrackets.test.tsx asserts brackets vanish when show_brackets:false (red before the render gate existed).

entries.title: Property names are folded (lowercase + space/underscore to hyphen) for display and matching so done_at and done-at are the same property, without rewriting the file (Logseq parity)
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (property.name-normalization, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (docs Properties.md:5-13 @08f855f; graph_parser/block.cljs:212-216 @680b371) folds a property key: lower-case then replace ' ' and '_' with '-', so done_at is the same property as done-at, and the rendered/effective name is the folded one. Tine (pre-fix on 197b226) compares keys with eq_ignore_ascii_case only (case fold, NO underscore fold) and renders the key verbatim (body.tsx renderProps), so done_at stays a distinct key from done-at and a done-at query/hidden-prop/alias match misses done_at::. Martin chose the DATA-SAFE fold: normalize the effective key for display + all matching, but never rewrite the file. Fail-before proven (manager-run): DocBlock::property("done-at") on `Done_At:: 1` returned None pre-fix; the round-trip of `Done_At:: 1` was already byte-exact and must stay so. Non-goals (follow-up): custom_id/custom-id->id and last-modified-at->updated-at alias remaps.

entries.title: Ctrl K shows a truncated-results affordance instead of implying a bounded result window is complete
entries.notes: Blocking stateful UX: each truncated Ctrl+K page or block category tells the user to narrow the query instead of implying the bounded window is complete. Existing result caps remain unchanged. (Autocomplete keep-typing hint deferred: it would require rerouting the [[/#/(( picker from QueryPlan::legacy_page_search to friendly, an unverified ranking change out of scope for GH #209.)

entries.title: A bare http(s) URL ending in a media extension auto-renders inline (image as , video/audio as player) like Logseq, while labeled links stay links
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (og-parity.assets-pdf-media.raw-remote-image-url, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). OG (og-1.0.0 components/block.cljs:989-1011 show-link? -> :1213 media-link; util/text.cljs:31 media-link?; formats config.cljs:118-127 + graph_parser/config.cljs:66) auto-renders a link as media when full_text starts with http(s):// AND the URL ends in a media extension (?query/#frag allowed): images gif/svg/jpeg/ico/png/jpg/bmp/webp, audio mp3/ogg/mpeg/wav/m4a/flac/wma/aac, video mp4/webm/mov/flv/avi/mkv. Tine renderLink (src/render/inline.tsx) rendered media only when the lsdoc node had image:true (![](url) syntax); a BARE https://host/pic.jpg link fell through to a plain <a class=external-link> (pre-fix). Fix: when !s.image AND s.full (raw source = OG full_text) starts with http(s):// AND mediaKind(dest)!=null, route to AssetImage (image) / MediaEmbed (video/audio) -- the components the s.image branch already uses. Discriminator on s.full (not label emptiness) is byte-faithful to OG: a labeled [text](x.png) has full '[text](x.png)' (not http-prefixed) and STAYS a link. Pure render change, no write path. Extension set = Tine's canonical mediaKind (media.ts) rather than OG's literal list (Tine adds avif/m4v/oga/opus; lacks ico) -- an intentional harmless presentation-heuristic difference, noted per AGENTS parity doctrine. Fail-before proven (manager-run).

entries.title: A {{youtube}}/{{video}} embed (and a pasted raw <iframe> to a video host) plays instead of failing with YouTube error 153; arbitrary pasted iframes keep no-referrer
entries.notes: OG-parity catalogue pick (og-parity.assets-pdf-media.youtube-embed-playback, LIVE-BACKLOG s9). A YouTube embed iframe that loads with NO referrer (or an opaque one) is rejected by YouTube's player as error 153. OG (og-1.0.0 6e7afa8eb extensions/video/youtube.cljs:54-70) builds the youtube iframe as https://www.youtube.com/embed/ID?enablejsapi=1 with allow='accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share' and referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' so the app origin reaches YouTube; vimeo (block.cljs:1290-1305) gets the same allow minus picture-in-picture/web-share and no referrerpolicy. Pre-fix Tine: VideoMacro (src/components/Macro.tsx) emitted a bare <iframe src=.../embed/ID allowfullscreen> with no referrerpolicy/allow/enablejsapi, and renderIframe (src/render/inline.tsx, the raw-HTML <iframe> path) hard-coded referrerpolicy='no-referrer' (sends nothing). Fix: VideoMacro youtube gets ?enablejsapi=1 + the OG allow list + referrerpolicy strict-origin-when-cross-origin; vimeo gets the OG allow list (no referrerpolicy); renderIframe sends strict-origin-when-cross-origin ONLY for known video hosts (youtube/youtube-nocookie/youtu.be/vimeo/loom/bilibili) and keeps no-referrer for every other pasted iframe (privacy preserved). Structural DOM parity proven by render tests (fail-before manager-run); actual WebKitGTK+network playback confirmation is real-app-pending (jsdom cannot load YouTube).
